sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented in available source

special_mechanics.name: Super Scope Light Gun Gameplay
special_mechanics.description: Primary mechanic: player controls Mario from first-person perspective on Yoshi's back and uses Super Scope light gun to shoot enemies. Power gauge depletes with firing and depletes faster with rapid fire, causing shot delays.

physics_values.shot_cooldown_scaling: dynamic based on power gauge depletion rate

special_mechanics.name: Automatic Yoshi Control (Single-Player)
special_mechanics.description: In single-player mode, Yoshi is controlled automatically. In multiplayer, one player controls Mario with Super Scope and another controls Yoshi with SNES controller.

tier_notes: Tier 3 - Empirical/Qualitative data only. Wikipedia source provides gameplay mechanics and context but contains no documented physics parameters, movement values, gravity constants, or sub-pixel system details. This is a light gun shooter requiring Super Scope peripheral, not a traditional platformer with detailed movement physics. Game focuses on shooting mechanics rather than character movement parameters.
